summary: Sprint 7A testing: ARC Credits backend APIs, ROI Clock on architect-upgrade page, Profile ARC section with store teaser, FlexCardGenerator in VictoryLapModal, and regression of home/discover/navigation. All 15 backend tests passed, all 9 profile ARC checks passed, ROI Clock verified, and regression tests clean.

minor.issue: Returns field 'arc_balance' (not 'balance' as stated in test requirements). Functionally correct but naming differs from spec.
get_next_arc_milestone returns 1000 when balance >= 1000 (should ideally signal 'max achieved'). Non-breaking.
No rate limiting or deduplication per event per user - users can earn unlimited ARC by calling same event multiple times. Potential business logic gap.

issues: Store items show ARC costs as plain numbers (500, 300, 750, 1000) with lock icon only - no 'ARC' label beside the number. Minor clarity issue.
Progress bar at 0% balance is barely visible (dark fill on dark background). Acceptable but low contrast.

action_items: Consider adding ARC per-event deduplication or rate limiting in POST /api/arc/award to prevent farming
Consider showing 'ARC' label next to store item cost numbers for clarity
server.py is now 1870 lines (well over 700-line guideline) - consider splitting ARC routes to a separate module
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1870 lines - exceeds 700-line file size guideline. ARC endpoints (lines 1813-1870) should be extracted to a separate router module.
Dead code in profile.tsx: 'const progress = ...' computed variable that is never used (only 'progressPct' is used in the render). Should be removed.
POST /api/arc/award has no event deduplication - same event can be awarded infinite times for same user. Risk of ARC farming if exposed to users.
ARC GET endpoint response key is 'arc_balance' while award response uses 'new_balance' - inconsistent naming within the same feature.
FlexCardGenerator.tsx uses 'captureRef' + Share for native but on web calls navigator.share or alerts - verify fallback works on desktop browsers
seed_data_creation: New test users created during test run: TEST_arc7_XXXXXX@blueprint.com (fresh user with 140 ARC after sequential award tests), TEST_arcthresh_XXXXXX@blueprint.com (100 ARC, Builder), TEST_arcstrat_XXXXXX@blueprint.com (300 ARC, Strategist)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint 7A complete. ARC system backend works perfectly. Profile ARC section renders correctly when user is logged in. ROI Clock verified on /architect-upgrade. FlexCardGenerator confirmed in VictoryLapModal celebrate stage via code review. For Playwright testing of logged-in state: inject user session via `localStorage.setItem('user', JSON.stringify(userObj))` (plain key 'user', NOT '@AsyncStorage:user' prefix). Login form does not work via Playwright keyboard/fill events due to React controlled input state issue - use localStorage injection instead. Test user arctest2@blueprint.com has 0 ARC + additional step/blueprint/share awards added during this run. Create fresh test users per test run for sequential ARC tests.
rca_of_issue: Playwright login form issue: React Native Web TextInput uses controlled components where state is managed by React hooks (setEmail/setPassword). Playwright's keyboard.type and fill methods update the DOM value but React's useState does not update because the nativeInputValueSetter approach requires input/change events to bubble up through React's synthetic event system. React 18 fiber reconciliation sometimes misses programmatic input value changes. Mitigation: Use localStorage.setItem('user', ...) to inject session data directly instead of going through the login form.
